description:
## Goal

Embed every chunk into a vector store with metadata sufficient for filtered hybrid retrieval. Runs partly in parallel with Stage 3 — Pass C of the graph stage depends on these embeddings, and the vector metadata is later backfilled with reviewed concept tags.

## Architecture

`embed_corpus.py` reads chunks from `corpus/`, embeds each `content.body` via the configured provider (Ollama / sentence-transformers / API), and upserts into the configured vector backend (ChromaDB or Qdrant) with full metadata (id, tradition, text_id, section, translator, token_count, concepts). Both the embedding model and vector store are abstracted so providers can be swapped via `config/embedding.toml`.

`backfill_concepts.py` is a separate idempotent pass that reads accepted EXPRESSES edges from `guru.db` and updates the `concepts` field on each vector — enabling filtered queries like "chunks tagged `emanation_hierarchy` semantically similar to this query".

## Outputs

- `config/embedding.toml` — model + backend + processing settings
- `data/vectordb/` — populated vector store
- Concept-tag metadata backfilled per vector after Stage 3 review

## Acceptance Criteria

- Vector count == chunk count exactly
- Metadata fields are complete and correctly typed
- Manual spot-check: query "divine light within all things" surfaces Gospel of Thomas Logion 77, Corpus Hermeticum, and an Upanishad passage
- Filtered search honors `tradition != X` exclusions
- Concept backfill correctly populates the `concepts` metadata field

## See Also

docs/guru-implementation.md §"Stage 4: Vector Indexing"
